description: Recursively flatten nested arrays into a single flat array
prompt: Write a JavaScript ES module that exports a function `flatten(arr)` which takes an arbitrarily nested array and returns a new flat array containing all elements in order. For example, `flatten([1, [2, [3, 4]], 5])` should return `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]`. Export as named export.

tests.name: flattens simple nested array
tests.code: const result = mod.flatten([1, [2, [3, 4]], 5]); assert(JSON.stringify(result) === JSON.stringify([1, 2, 3, 4, 5]), 'Expected [1,2,3,4,5] but got ' + JSON.stringify(result));

tests.name: returns already flat array unchanged
tests.code: const result = mod.flatten([1, 2, 3]); assert(JSON.stringify(result) === JSON.stringify([1, 2, 3]), 'Expected [1,2,3] but got ' + JSON.stringify(result));

tests.name: handles empty array
tests.code: const result = mod.flatten([]); assert(JSON.stringify(result) === JSON.stringify([]), 'Expected [] but got ' + JSON.stringify(result));

tests.name: handles deeply nested arrays
tests.code: const result = mod.flatten([[[[[1]]]]]);  assert(JSON.stringify(result) === JSON.stringify([1]), 'Expected [1] but got ' + JSON.stringify(result));

tests.name: handles mixed types including strings and nulls
tests.code: const result = mod.flatten([1, ['a', [null, [true]]], 'b']); assert(JSON.stringify(result) === JSON.stringify([1, 'a', null, true, 'b']), 'Expected [1,"a",null,true,"b"] but got ' + JSON.stringify(result));
